tool.name: SNP summary statistics
tool.description: estimate He, Hobs, Fis and allelic richness.
tool.command:
mkdir -p summary_file && mkdir -p matrices_output &&
Rscript '$__tool_directory__/summ_div_stats.R' '$data_conditional.marker_type' '$data_conditional.input_data' '$data_conditional.input_data.element_identifier' #if $data_conditional.marker_type == "snp": '$data_conditional.indpop' #end if

param.name: input_data
param.label: Upload your VCF file
param.help: File containing SNP data in VCF format. You can upload a simple file, multiple file or a dataset collection

param.name: indpop
param.label: Upload your population file
param.help: File containing individuals in the first column and their population in the second, separated by a tabulation character.

param.name: input_data
param.label: Upload your SSR data file
param.help: File containing SSR/microsatellite data in tabular format.

tool.help:
Usage =====
As input, the user provides genetic data depending on the marker type selected. For SNPs, one or more VCF files (Danecek *et al.* 2011) and a population file (see format below). For SSRs, one or more tabular files containing genotypes and population information (see format below).
This tool computes standard genetic diversity statistics and differentiation indicies using several R packages (adegenet, hierfstat and mmod). It calculates observed and expected heterozygosity (Hobs, Hexp), allelic richness (Ar), and inbreeding coefficient (Fis) (Weir & Cockerham 1984). It also performs pairwise population comparisons to provide Fst (Weir & Cockerham 1984), Gst (Nei 1973) and Jost's D statistics (Jost 2008).
The tool generate a summary table with results per population, the average across populations (mean) and the results for all individuals pooled (tot). Additionnally, it outputs the full pairwise diferentiation matrices in a dataset collection.
Examples files ============== **Population file (tab):**
::
ind1 pop1 ind2 pop2 ind3 pop2 ind4 pop1
**SSR Genotype file (tab):**
::
Ind Pop Loc1 Loc2 Loc3 ind1 pop1 120/124 200/202 150/150 ind2 pop1 120/120 202/204 150/152 ind3 pop2 124/126 200/200 152/152
**Summary output table:**
+----------+------+-------------+-------+-------+-------+------+-------+----------+--------------+-------------+ | Dataset | Pop | Marker_type | n_ind | Hobs | Hexp | Ar | Fis | mean_Fst | mean_Gst_Nei | mean_Jost_D | +==========+======+=============+=======+=======+=======+======+=======+==========+==============+=============+ | MyStudy | Pop1 | SNP | 25 | 0.320 | 0.345 | 1.85 | 0.072 | 0.045 | 0.038 | 0.012 | +----------+------+-------------+-------+-------+-------+------+-------+----------+--------------+-------------+ | MyStudy | Pop2 | SNP | 30 | 0.310 | 0.330 | 1.78 | 0.060 | 0.048 | 0.041 | 0.015 | +----------+------+-------------+-------+-------+-------+------+-------+----------+--------------+-------------+ | MyStudy | tot | SNP | 55 | 0.315 | 0.337 | 1.82 | 0.066 | NA | NA | NA | +----------+------+-------------+-------+-------+-------+------+-------+----------+--------------+-------------+ | MyStudy | mean | SNP | 55 | 0.315 | 0.337 | 1.81 | 0.066 | 0.046 | 0.039 | 0.013 | +----------+------+-------------+-------+-------+-------+------+-------+----------+--------------+-------------+
**Pairwise matrix output (example for Fst):**
::
pop1 pop2 pop3 pop1 0.000 0.045 0.062 pop2 0.045 0.000 0.038 pop3 0.062 0.038 0.000
See also examples of VCF formats in : https://samtools.github.io/hts-specs/VCFv4.5.pdf

citations.citation: @Manual{dplyr, title = {dplyr: A Grammar of Data Manipulation}, author = {Hadley Wickham and Romain François and Lionel Henry and Kirill Müller and Davis Vaughan and Posit Software, PBC}, year = {2016}, note = {R package version 0.5.0}, doi = {10.32614/CRAN.package.dplyr}, url = {https://dplyr.tidyverse.org}, publisher = {Comprehensive R Archive Network (CRAN)}, }

@Manual{vcfR, title = {vcfR: Manipulate and Visualize VCF Data}, author = {Brian J. Knaus and Niklaus J. Grunwald and Eric C. Anderson and David J. Winter and Zhian N. Kamvar and Javier F. Tabima}, year = {2018}, note = {R package version 1.8.0}, doi = {10.32614/CRAN.package.vcfR}, url = {https://github.com/knausb/vcfR}, publisher = {Comprehensive R Archive Network (CRAN)}, }

@article{Danecek2011, author = {Danecek, Petr and Auton, Adam and Abecasis, Goncalo and Albers, Cornelis A. and Banks, Erica and DePristo, Mark A. and Handsaker, Robert E. and Lunter, Gerton and Marth, Gabor T. and Sherry, Stephen T. and McVean, Gil and Durbin, Richard and {1000 Genomes Project Analysis Group}}, title = {The variant call format and {VCF}tools}, journal = {Bioinformatics}, volume = {27}, number = {15}, pages = {2156-2158}, year = {2011}, doi = {10.1093/bioinformatics/btr330}, URL = {https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/btr330}, eprint = {https://academic.oup.com/bioinformatics/article-pdf/27/15/2156/600788/btr330.pdf}, }

@article{DrayDufour2007, author = {Dray, Stéphane and Dufour, Anne-Béatrice}, title = {The ade4 Package: Implementing the Duality Diagram for Ecologists}, journal = {Journal of Statistical Software}, year = {2007}, volume = {22}, number = {4}, pages = {1--20}, doi = {10.18637/jss.v022.i04}, url = {https://doi.org/10.18637/jss.v022.i04} }
@Article{Winter2012, title = {mmod: an R library for the calculation of population differentiation statistics}, author = {D.J. Winter}, journal = {Molecular Ecology Resources}, year = {2012},

@article{nei1973analysis, author = {Nei, M.}, title = {Analysis of Gene Diversity in Subdivided Populations}, journal = {Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences}, volume = {70}, number = {12}, pages = {3321--3323}, year = {1973}, doi = {10.1073/pnas.70.12.3321}, url = {https://www.pnas.org/doi/abs/10.1073/pnas.70.12.3321} }

@article{jost2008gst, author = {Jost, Lou}, title = {$G_{ST}$ and its relatives do not measure differentiation}, journal = {Molecular Ecology}, volume = {17}, number = {18}, pages = {4015--4026}, doi = {10.1111/j.1365-294X.2008.03887.x}, url = {https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.1111/j.1365-294X.2008.03887.x}, year = {2008} }

@article{weir1984estimating, author = {Weir, Bruce S. and Cockerham, C. Clark}, title = {Estimating F-Statistics for the Analysis of Population Structure}, journal = {Evolution}, volume = {38}, number = {6}, pages = {1358--1370}, year = {1984}, doi = {10.2307/2408641}, url = {https://www.jstor.org/stable/2408641} }
